article_title: US Stock Market | S&P 500, Dow end lower as escalating Iran war sours risk appetite
summary_v1: US stocks, including the S&P 500 and Dow, ended lower due to escalating tensions between the US and Iran, which dampened risk appetite despite a calm inflation report. Investors were concerned about rising oil prices affecting consumer spending. Meanwhile, Indian markets also reacted negatively to the global downturn amid geopolitical uncertainties.
summary_v2: US stocks closed lower on Wednesday as escalating tensions between the US and Iran overshadowed a calm inflation report. Investors grew concerned about rising oil prices and their impact on consumer spending. Saudi Arabia increased oil production amid the heightened geopolitical risks, contributing to market uncertainty. Indian markets also reacted negatively to the global downturn.

article_title: Atlassian cuts hundreds of Australian jobs as AI slashes labour needs

summary_v1: The article discusses the phenomenon of spontaneous human combustion, a topic often regarded with skepticism and fascination, especially among those familiar with late 20th-century mysteries. It hints at the cultural intrigue and myths surrounding the event but does not provide detailed causes or scientific explanations.
summary_v2: Spontaneous human combustion (SHC) refers to the unexplained phenomenon where a person suddenly bursts into flames without an external source of ignition. Despite its popularity in late 20th-century folklore, SHC remains scientifically unproven and is often attributed to other causes like underlying medical conditions or external factors. Understanding SHC is important to dispel myths and focus on real fire safety measures.

summary_v1: Elon Musk unveiled a joint Tesla-xAI project called "Macrohard" or "Digital Optimus," designed to simulate software creations like those from Microsoft. The system will operate using Tesla's in-house AI4 chip combined with xAI's Nvidia-based server hardware, aiming to disrupt the software industry.
summary_v2: Elon Musk unveiled "Macrohard," a joint AI project between Tesla and his startup xAI, designed to simulate software development similar to companies like Microsoft. article_title: Grammarly shuts down “Expert Review” after pushback from the real experts
summary_v1: Grammarly has shut down its "Expert Review" feature following backlash from journalists, academics, and authors. The feature allowed users to improve their writing by selecting from a roster of experts, but was criticized for impersonating writers. Superhuman Platform Inc. decided to remove the feature after the negative response.
